集训3 · 假期复习任务卡 · Day 8 of 24

The Scavenger Hunt

📖 Reading

310 words · 27 target words · listen while you read

Tier 1（11词） Tier 2（4词） Tier 3（12词）

Zara saw a bright sign taped to the park notice board. It announced a Saturday scavenger hunt adventure through the neighborhood, sponsored every year by a rich man who lived at the top of the hill. Spots were available for any team, and the prize was a shiny new bike.

Zara felt eager to join, so she called her best friend, Nia. Nia's mom encouraged them to enter, even though the weekend was already busy with soccer games and piano lessons.

On Saturday, a small crowd gathered near the park gate. The organizer blew a loud whistle as a signal, and the hunt began. Zara and Nia were ready, clutching a list of five clues.

The first clue seemed easy: solve a simple riddle and it would send them toward the old stone fountain. They crossed a wide road, careful of cars, and soon reached the fountain. Tucked beneath a rock nearby was a thin folded note with the second clue.

Through the quiet park they went next, laughing at a playful puppy that kept following them. Along the trail, they heard a strange noise in the bushes — only a rabbit hopping away. Zara stopped to tie her shoe before they hurried on.

At last, the final clue led them back to the park gate, where they had to find a small silver key hidden inside a flowerpot. Zara spotted it first and grabbed it with a shout. They were not alone in finishing — three other teams arrived at almost the same moment.

The organizer gathered everyone and, in front of the whole audience, read out the winning team's name. It was not Zara and Nia, but they did not mind. The sponsor came down to present each team with a small treat bag for trying so hard, and Zara promised to return next year to try again.

🔍 Reading Comprehension

5 questions · tap to check your answer

1In "Spots were available for any team, and the prize was a shiny new bike", what does available mean?
B — available means able to be used or obtained, so any team could still join the hunt.
2In "The first clue seemed easy: solve a simple riddle and it would send them toward the old stone fountain", what does seemed mean?
A — seem means to appear a certain way, so the clue looked easy at first glance.
3In "Tucked beneath a rock nearby was a thin folded note with the second clue", what does thin mean?
B — thin means not thick or wide, describing how slim the folded note was.
4What did Zara and Nia have to do to complete the scavenger hunt?
B — the girls followed a list of clues that led them to find a small silver key hidden in a flowerpot.
5How did Zara and Nia feel about not winning the scavenger hunt?
B — the story says they did not mind losing, and Zara promised to return next year to try again.
🔁
